description: A podcast for educators making sense of AI without turning school into a tech circus. Preparing students for their future, not our past. Part of the AGI Podcast network.

episodes.title: You Can't Shape What You Won't Use: Anne Leftwich on AI at Indiana University
episodes.summary: IU's Anne Leftwich on why AI holdouts forfeit their seat at the table, GenAI 101 for everyone, and why K-12 bans fail like abstinence-only everything.

episodes.title: Student-Led AI in Schools: Richmond Tech Team on MagicSchool, AI Literacy & Future Jobs
episodes.summary: Jeremy Hill's Richmond HS tech team red-teamed MagicSchool for two weeks, and now students run the district's AI rollout.

episodes.title: AI Jobs for CS Grads in 2026: Why Startups Beat Big Tech
episodes.summary: Purdue CS junior Bohyeon Jang on the frozen Big Tech entry-level market and why AI-fluent grads should target startups and SMBs instead.

episodes.title: Starting AI Conversations in Elementary School
episodes.summary: Zionsville digital learning coach Stevie Frank on age-appropriate AI conversations from kindergarten up, in a district that never blocked ChatGPT.

episodes.title: Resumes Are Dead. Here's What CS Students Should Do Instead
episodes.summary: Purdue's Ethan Dawes, the "Can AI do this?" sign guy, on why mass-applying is broken and what portfolios, oral exams, and AI scaffolding fix.

episodes.title: Magic School AI vs ChatGPT: Safer AI for K-12 Classrooms
episodes.summary: CIESC's Tanika Kinartail on how vertical AI handles students differently than ChatGPT and train-the-trainer PD for districts with no budget.

episodes.title: Alpha School: Kids Learn 2X Faster in 2 Hours a Day
episodes.summary: Alpha School's Sofia Swanson walks through the 2 Hour Learning model, top-1% MAP claims, and the selection-bias question, with co-host Ronda Swartz.

episodes.title: Grade the Prompt, Not Just the Paper: AI in College Classrooms
episodes.summary: Ivy Tech's Mark Lowe is piloting grading the AI prompt alongside the paper, treating AI as tutor and debate partner instead of policing it.

episodes.title: How Should Teachers Use AI Without Killing Critical Thinking?
episodes.summary: Hunter Hickman returns: AI makes students faster, but is it making them better thinkers, and how a rural district trains teachers either way.

episodes.title: Are Teachers Ready to Teach AI? Indiana's Answer
episodes.summary: Ryan Murray on building Indiana's AI teacher certification from scratch and what readiness actually looks like.

episodes.title: AI Literacy Is a Workforce Requirement. Are K-12 Schools Ready?
episodes.summary: Kristen Kayser on what AI literacy actually means for K-12 students and why most districts are getting it wrong.

episodes.title: How Community Colleges Are Building AI Workforce Pathways in 2026
episodes.summary: Ivy Tech's Amanda Wilkerson on how community colleges are building AI workforce pathways ahead of the four-years.

episodes.title: How College Students Should Use AI to Get Hired in 2026
episodes.summary: How college students should actually use AI in the job hunt without outsourcing the thinking that gets them hired.

episodes.title: Why AI Adoption Fails Without Behavior Change
episodes.summary: Nick Bonora on why AI rollouts stall for the same reason most conferences are forgettable: nobody designed for behavior change.

episodes.title: Middle School Teacher Uses AI to Save Time and Know Students Better
episodes.summary: Language arts teacher Patty Oshier on using AI to build better lessons, claw back time, and actually know her students better.

episodes.title: ChatGPT in Paramedic School: How One EMT Stopped Reading Textbooks
episodes.summary: Jordan Meyers on trading the textbook grind for ChatGPT study workflows in paramedic school.

episodes.title: AI Can't Replace Your Music Teacher (But It Should Help)
episodes.summary: Future Proof Music School founder John von Seggern on where AI belongs in music education and where the human teacher stays.

episodes.title: What Employers Actually Want: AI Skills + People Skills
episodes.summary: Transformation coach Lena Morris on pairing AI fluency with the people skills employers keep saying they can't find.

episodes.title: Why This Is AI's 1980s Garage Moment for Students
episodes.summary: A Columbia grad turned Wall Street quant turned AI entrepreneur on why students are living the 1980s garage moment and need digital literacy first.

episodes.title: AI and Writing Instruction: A 31-Year Teacher on AI Literacy
episodes.summary: Patty McGee brings 31 years of classroom experience to AI and writing instruction, testing tools with students and pushing back on detection.

episodes.title: AI in High School Healthcare: Running Real Patient Scenarios Through ChatGPT
episodes.summary: Teaching Tulsa high schoolers to run real healthcare scenarios through ChatGPT, with paramedic instructor Rebekah Dearing.

episodes.title: Navigating AI in Education: Insights from MIT
episodes.summary: MIT PhD candidate BreAnne Fleer on curious skepticism over blind adoption, what the Luddites actually opposed, and why coding literacy still matters.

episodes.title: District-Wide AI in K-5: The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly Truth
episodes.summary: Courtney Bock trained AI chatbots in 2010, became a K-5 educator, and now leads district-wide AI implementation with no illusions.

episodes.title: Are Schools Keeping Up With AI's Job Shifts? (Carmel Mayor Sue Finkam)
episodes.summary: Carmel Mayor Sue Finkam and Invest Hamilton County's Mike Thibideau get pressed on whether schools are keeping pace with AI's job shifts.

episodes.title: The Data Skills Gap: Why Healthcare and Education Need Data Science Grads Now
episodes.summary: Julian Yoon explains data lakes with laundry baskets and why healthcare and education need data science grads now.

episodes.title: Is the Education System Failing Students? AI, Isolation, and Mental Health
episodes.summary: Dr. KaT Zarychta on the global student mental-health crisis, 1 in 3 young adults feeling isolated, and AI as ally rather than threat.

episodes.title: The Teacher-Built AI Tool in 130+ Countries: Eduaide.ai
episodes.summary: Eduaide.ai founder Thomas Hummel on building a teacher AI tool from a Title I classroom to a million educators in 130+ countries.

episodes.title: Rolling Out School AI to Hesitant Teachers (and Why Catholic Schools Are Ready)
episodes.summary: "Dr. Bubbles" Ronda Swartz on rolling out School AI to hesitant teachers and why Catholic schools may be positioned for the AI era.

episodes.title: Rural AI Success: How a Small Indiana School District Actually Implemented Khanmigo
episodes.summary: Hunter Hickman on how Rensselaer Central hit 92% student activation with Khanmigo while big districts were still debating policy.

episodes.title: Will AI Take Your Job? A Purdue Senior on Networking Over Panic
episodes.summary: A Purdue industrial engineering senior on landing offers through networking and why complacency, not automation, is the real career threat.

episodes.title: AI in Special Education: From Grading Piles to Facilitators
episodes.summary: Former elementary and special-ed teacher Mary Dougherty on AI turning teachers into facilitators and opening doors for special-needs students.

episodes.title: The TikTok Generation's AI Problem: Climate Myths and Career Reality
episodes.summary: Kimba Rund returns to debunk the viral AI water-bottle myth and explain what her generation actually thinks about AI careers.

episodes.title: "I Didn't Know a Fourth Grader Could Change the World": AI in K-12
episodes.summary: Solutions architect Karisa Schwanekamp on the pandemic moment that changed her teaching and learning alongside students with AI.

episodes.title: Teaching Students to Control AI: Purdue's QUIZ Method (Dr. Kathryn Dilworth)
episodes.summary: Purdue's Kathryn Dilworth on her QUIZ methodology for teaching students to control AI instead of being controlled by it.

episodes.title: AI Ethics: Why We Should Parent AI, Not Just Build It (De Kai)
episodes.summary: AI pioneer De Kai argues we should stop treating AI as machines and start parenting them as artificial children.

episodes.title: The Entrepreneurial Professor: Bridging Academia and Business Reality
episodes.summary: Susan Woods on AI adoption across generations and why human oversight and critical thinking stay non-negotiable.

episodes.title: How Community Colleges Build AI Workforce Pathways (Ivy Tech's Brooklyn Burton)
episodes.summary: Launch episode: Ivy Tech Lafayette Vice Chancellor Brooklyn Burton on AI-driven workforce and economic development.

episodes.title: AGI - School's Out Saturdays PRE-LAUNCH PEP-RALLY (Episode 25)
episodes.summary: Pre-launch special with transformation coach Lena Morris on AI adoption and human behavioral change in education.
